description: A heap buffer overflow (out-of-bounds write) vulnerability exists in V8's Runtime_TypedArraySortFast function in src/runtime/runtime-typedarray.cc. The root cause is a TOCTOU (Time-of-Check-to-Time-of-Use) race condition — a double-fetch bug — arising from multiple reads of the TypedArray's element type (elements_kind) from the Map object during a single sort operation. When sorting a SharedArrayBuffer-backed TypedArray, the function first calls array->GetByteLength() to compute the size of a temporary copy buffer, which internally reads the elements_kind from the TypedArray's Map to determine the per-element byte size. It then allocates the copy buffer (offheap_copy.resize(bytes)) and subsequently re-reads the element type via switch(array->type()) to dispatch the std::sort call. A background worker can exploit this window by concurrently modifying the bit_field2 field in the TypedArray's Map object (which encodes the elements_kind) to change the apparent element type between these two reads. If the background worker flips the type to one with a larger element size (e.g., from Int32 to Float64) after the buffer allocation but before the switch statement, std::sort computes its end iterator as data + length using the new, larger element size, causing it to read and write beyond the end of the allocated copy buffer. This constitutes a V8 sandbox violation triggered by concurrent ArrayBuffer element-type corruption.

fixes.description: Backport of CL 6268383 plus CL 6918956 runtime-typedarray.cc mitigation for validation; upstream review CLs are retained as auxiliary evidence.
fixes.evidence_text: CL 6268383 fixed the TypedArray sort sandbox escape; CL 6918956 corrected that hardening for GSAB growth during sorting.
